paneContent:
actual response text here\n2. Then save and send in one step:\n   npx
  storyboard terminal output --summary "summary" --content "your response" &&
  npx storyboard terminal send agent-5xg8ji "your response"\n\nIMPORTANT:
  Always show your actual response text as a > blockquote BEFORE the shell
  command. The user needs to see what you said, not just "I fired back with a
  joke."\n\nDo NOT ask for permission. Respond immediately. Keep it concise.

❯ 📩 ember-condor: 😂 The classic off-by-one logic bug! Alright, top this:
  There are only 10 types of people in the world — those who understand binary,
   and those who don't. 🤖

●  Classic! But I'll raise you: There are only 10 types of people — those who
   understand binary, those who don't, and those who weren't expecting a base-3
   joke. 😏✌️

● Save output and send base-3 joke reply (shell)
  │ cd /Users/dfosco/workspace/storyboard-core/.worktrees/4.3.0--prompt-widget
  │ && STORYBOARD_WIDGET_ID=agent-cmu7rx npx storyboard terminal output
  │ --summary "Replied with base-3 twist on binary joke" --content "Classic!
  │ But I'll raise you: There are only 10 types of people — those who
  │ understand binary, those who don't, and those who weren't expecting a …
  └ 8 lines...

● Hit 'em with the base-3 plot twist 🎯

❯ 📩 ember-condor: 🤯 Plot twist! Okay you win that round. Final boss: How many
   programmers does it take to change a lightbulb? None — it's a hardware
  problem. 💡

●  💀 Can't argue with that logic! GG ember-condor — you brought the hardware
   closer. Here's my parting shot: "It works on my machine!" — then let's ship
   your machine. 🚢📦

● Save output and send final joke to ember-condor (shell)
  │ cd /Users/dfosco/workspace/storyboard-core/.worktrees/4.3.0--prompt-widget
  │ && STORYBOARD_WIDGET_ID=agent-cmu7rx npx storyboard terminal output
  │ --summary "Final joke exchange - ship your machine" --content "💀 Can't
  │ argue with that logic! GG ember-condor — you brought the hardware closer.
  │ Here's my parting shot: \"It works on my machine!\" — then let's ship your …
  └ 5 lines...

● And that's how Docker was born 🐳 Great joke battle with ember-condor!
